content: Just as 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems prioritize retaining only high-information, uncertainty-reducing experience chunks to avoid catastrophic forgetting and improve sample efficiency, the emergent behavioral identity of a long-lived agent is shaped by the same active pruning and prioritization rule: the agent ‘actively learns’ which self-experiences to retain in its long-term behavioral memory to optimize future adaptive interaction, rather than storing a complete record of all past actions.
